content.full_text:
original

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5562          322-5560          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

November 25, 1980


Ronald W. Beals
Attorney at Law
1007 Seventh St., Suite 500
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Beals:

I am writing in response to your letter of November 13,
1980, which refers to your telephone inquiry of March 10,
1980, about the Art Snyder Legal Defense Fund. As I under-
stand it, this fund has been set up solely to defray Council-
man Snyder's costs in defending himself against charges which do
not involve his status as a candidate or his duties as a
councilman. Under these circumstances, contributions to the
fund are not campaign contributions, and need not be reported
as campaign contributions by the donor.

Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215(b)
provides a guideline for determining whether a payment is or
is not a campaign contribution:

(b) "Contribution" includes any monetary or
nonmonetary payment for which full and adequate con-
sideration is not made to the donor that is received
by or made at the behest of:

(1) A candidate, unless it is clear from
surrounding circumstances that the payment was
received or made at his behest for personal
purposes unrelated to his candidacy or status
as an office holder. . . .

The legal defense fund, as you described it, is set up for a
purpose which is unrelated to Councilman Snyder's candidacy
or to his status as an officeholder. Therefore donations to
this fund are not campaign contributions under the Political
Reform Act. See also the Commission Opinion in the Matter
of Buchanan, 5 FPPC Opinions 14 (May 1, 1979).

/82/5(b)

Ronald W. Beals
November 25, 1980
Page Two


     Although donations to Councilman Snyder's legal defense
fund do not have to be reported as campaign contributions,
they do have to be reported on the Councilman's annual state-
ment of economic interests as gifts if they exceed $25,
unless they come within one of the exceptions listed in
Government Code Section 82028(b). See Government Code Section
87207. In addition, Councilman Snyder will be required to
disqualify himself from acting in his official capacity on
matters involving any person who has made a gift of $250 or
more to his legal defense fund. Government Code Sections
87100, 87103(c).

     I hope that this answers your questions. If you have
any further questions, please feel free to call me.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Staff Counsel
                                        Legal Division

BAM:plh

RONALD W. BEALS
Attorney at Law
1007 SEVENTH ST
SUITE 500
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

916-441-6735

November 13, 1980


Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Div.
1100 K Street
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Art Snyder Legal Defense Fund

Dear Sirs:

This will confirm my November 10, 1980, phone conversation
with your office that donations to the Art Snyder Legal
Defense Fund, to be used only to defer Councilman Snyder's
legal expenses, are not considered a campaign contribution
and need not be reported by any party donating to this
Legal Defense Fund.

Thank you very much.

Very truly yours,


RONALD W. BEALS


RWB/mlr

P.S. As I've not heard from your office since November 13, 1980,
when I was informed this matter would be the subject of
legal review, I assume this original opinion has not been
changed.

bcc: Jim Sample

Beals Attorney at Law 1007 Seventh St., Suite 500 Sacramento, CA 95814 Dear Mr. Beals: I am writing in response to your letter of November 13, 1980, which refers to your telephone inquiry of March 10, 1980, about the Art Snyder Legal Defense Fund. As I understand it, this fund has been set up solely to defray Councilman Snyder's costs in defending himself against charges which do not involve his status as a candidate or his duties as a councilman. Under these circumstances, contributions to the fund are not campaign contributions, and need not be reported as campaign contributions by the donor. Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18215(b) provides a guideline for determining whether a payment is or is not a campaign contribution: (b) "Contribution" includes any monetary or nonmonetary payment for which full and adequate consideration is not made to the donor that is received by or made at the behest of: (1) A candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding circumstances that the payment was received or made at his behest for personal purposes unrelated to his candidacy or status as an office holder. The legal defense fund, as you described it, is set up for a purpose which is unrelated to Councilman Snyder's candidacy or to his status as an officeholder. Therefore donations to this fund are not campaign contributions under the Political Reform Act. See also the Commission Opinion in the Matter of Buchanan, 5 FPPC Opinions 14 (May 1, 1979). Although donations to Councilman Snyder's legal defense fund do not have to be reported as campaign contributions, they do have to be reported on the Councilman's annual statement of economic interests as gifts if they exceed $25, unless they come within one of the exceptions listed in Government Code Section 82028(b). See Government Code Section 87207. In addition, Councilman Snyder will be required to disqualify himself from acting in his official capacity on matters involving any person who has made a gift of $250 or more to his legal defense fund. Government Code Sections 87100, 87103(c). I hope that this answers your questions. If you have any further questions, please feel free to call me. Very truly yours, [signature] Barbara A.
